old_claim.note: Interchangeable Parts provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Interchangeable-parts production depended on tolerances, gauges, and repeatable inspection practices that fed directly into industrial quality-control practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines interchangeable parts as identical components and links mass production of identical parts to precision equipment and machine-tool practice.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source connects interchangeable parts with precision production of identical components.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is enabling because interchangeable-parts production provides the tolerance and repeatability problem space that quality control manages.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: interchangeable_parts remains an enabling edge with textbook support.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed solely to 20th-century statistical process control.
A dedicated industrial_metrology node is added and should replace interchangeable_parts.
A stronger source shows quality-control practice should be modeled only downstream of inspection gauges.
short_reason: Interchangeable parts supply the repeatability/tolerance context.
